summary: FQ-02 background trees and pass-through flora: each tree site now becomes either a solid, mineable foreground wood column (unchanged) or a pass-through background tree (bg_trunk/bg_canopy) rendered on a new BackgroundFlora TileMapLayer behind blocks and actors with no physics/occlusion layers and a dim tint. New generation.tree_foreground_ratio (default 0.4, world-builder slider) controls the split, with a forced foreground tree after 2 consecutive background trees. Background flora is pure visual state: deterministic from seed+config, never saved, never in cells, in-bounds guarded, cleared across the hall footprint; bushes skip background cells. Smoke 134 -> 142 including a live walk-past traversal check; mining/axe/bush/save-load contracts re-verified unchanged. Orchestrated as Explore recon + orchestrator implementation + independent Windows verification + sonnet review; review's two should-fix findings (negative-y stamping, vacuous solid sub-check) fixed and re-verified green before commit.
scope: FQ-02 only, per docs/FABLE_TASK_QUEUE.md. Implementation commit 434c7ae plus this evidence-only packet commit.
next_action: FQ-03: equipment data model and character-owned gear slots.
